privacy.h1: Privacy policy and local data boundary.

labels.Privacy disconnected services: What is not connected hereThis app shell does not connect WHOOP, accounts, hosted AI, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, or production backend writes.
labels.Privacy local product data: Local product dataThe higher-functionality demo can store a daily plan, missed-day quick restart, midday rescue, evening close, kept or rejected memory candidates, pattern decisions, one local experiment, its observation log, its local decision note, local day history checkpoints, and pasted local export restores. These are browser-local records.
labels.Privacy reachability: ReachabilityLinked from app chromePrivacy is reachable from the top bar, desktop side navigation, and Profile controls.
labels.Privacy wellness boundary: Wellness and safety claimsLifeMax is for personal wellness reflection. It does not diagnose, treat, prescribe, or replace qualified medical advice.

visualInspectionAddendum.reason: Initial mobile screenshots showed top-of-page or clicked state; added focused shots for loaded sample capture list and local trend/history section.

objective: Make the public LifeMax app visibly higher-functionality by adding a browser-local sample week that immediately populates Today, Capture, Patterns, Experiments, Profile, and Privacy without fake integration claims.
truthBoundary: No WHOOP, accounts, hosted AI, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Postgres, backend writes, reminders, timers, or cross-device sync are connected or claimed.
Lab/shared-context LifeMax boot reports are red, so daily usable connected LifeMax is not claimed.

defectsFound: Visual test expected a Today summary phrase that was not an actual accessible UI contract.
Privacy panels were reachable but lacked stable section labels for proof/accessibility.
Initial screenshots did not show the most representative Capture list and Pattern trend surfaces.
repairsMade: Aligned visual test expectations to real Source freshness and Capture list accessible names.
Added aria labels to Privacy policy sections and included Privacy in the sample-week visual path.
Captured focused mobile screenshots for the sample Capture list and Pattern trend/history section.

environmentBlockers: Standalone Playwright mobile screenshot command is not used in this environment because the Codex parent process has a large <defunct> child backlog and Chromium launch previously timed out. In-app browser smoke is the current browser proof path.

rollbackPath: Git rollback path: revert the commit that adds the sample-week loader and privacy proof labels, then redeploy the previous Vercel production commit if needed.
Runtime rollback path: Vercel dashboard/CLI can promote the prior production deployment; no database/auth/credential migration is part of this slice.
User data rollback is not needed for backend records because this feature writes only browser-local demo state.

postActionEvidence.local: Local post-action proof passed; deployment post-action smoke pending until Vercel deploy finishes.

noSecretsHandling: No secrets printed or edited. Diff scan for sk-/secret/token/password/api-key/bearer patterns returned no matches.
